req.title: Audit log access (audit)
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three distinct verification concerns: (1) a privacy-officer can successfully retrieve AuditEvent records scoped to their own organisation (positive / EP), (2) a privacy-officer is forbidden from retrieving AuditEvent records belonging to a different organisation (negative / NEG), and (3) the AuditEvent emission itself is verified end-to-end at the boundary where exactly one new event is created per operation (boundary / BVA). A fourth test covers the decision-table combination of an unauthenticated caller, strengthening the auth matrix. Each test uses shared fixtures for token acquisition and HTTP session setup, keeping individual test bodies under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_retrieves_own_org_audit_events
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for org_id=ORG_A
At least one AuditEvent record exists for ORG_A
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer for ORG_A to obtain a bearer token
expected: Response body is a FHIR Bundle with resourceType=Bundle
trace: R-HLT-089: privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_privacy_officer_retrieves_own_org_audit_events(
    api_session,          # <fixture> requests.Session with base_url set
    officer_token_org_a,  # <fixture> bearer token for ORG_A privacy-officer
    org_a_id,             # <fixture> str constant for ORG_A identifier
    seed_audit_event_org_a,  # <fixture> ensures >=1 AuditEvent for ORG_A
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_session.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": org_a_id},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert body.get("total", 0) >= 1
    for entry in body.get("entry", []):
        resource = entry["resource"]
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        orgs = [a["who"]["reference"] for a in agents if "who" in a]
        assert any(org_a_id in ref for ref in orgs), (
            f"Entry {resource.get('id')} not scoped to {org_a_id}"
        )

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_events
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for org_id=ORG_A
A separate organisation org_id=ORG_B exists with its own AuditEvent records
The privacy-officer for ORG_A has NO grants on ORG_B
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer for ORG_A to obtain a bearer token
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome with issue.severity=error
No AuditEvent resources from ORG_B are disclosed
trace: R-HLT-089: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden

tests.name: test_single_operation_emits_exactly_one_audit_event
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists for org_id=ORG_A
A known count of AuditEvent records exists for ORG_A before the test (count_before)
The fhir-lite service records an AuditEvent for every /AuditEvent GET
expected: HTTP 200 on both queries and the tested operation
count_after equals count_before + 1 (exactly one new AuditEvent emitted)
trace: R-HLT-089: An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_single_operation_emits_exactly_one_audit_event(
    api_session,
    officer_token_org_a,
    org_a_id,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}

    def fetch_total():
        r = api_session.get(
            "/AuditEvent",
            params={"organization": org_a_id},
            headers=headers,
        )
        assert r.status_code == 200
        return r.json().get("total", len(r.json().get("entry", [])))

    count_before = fetch_total()
    # The operation under test — one explicit GET
    op_resp = api_session.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": org_a_id},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert op_resp.status_code == 200
    time.sleep(0.5)  # allow async event persistence
    count_after = fetch_total()
    # BVA: boundary at exactly +1; neither 0 nor 2 is acceptable
    assert count_after == count_before + 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 new AuditEvent; "
        f"before={count_before}, after={count_after}"
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_denied_with_401
preconditions: The fhir-lite service requires authentication on /AuditEvent
actions: Send GET /AuditEvent?organization=ORG_A without any Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or WWW-Authenticate header
trace: R-HLT-089: role:privacy-officer precondition — absent credentials must be rejected
R-HLT-089: auth category — all non-authenticated paths must fail closed
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_denied_with_401(
    api_session,
    org_a_id,
):
    # DT: decision column — authenticated=False, role=None => deny
    resp = api_session.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": org_a_id},
        # deliberately omit Authorization header
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    has_www_auth = "WWW-Authenticate" in resp.headers
    has_outcome = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").startswith("application/fhir")
    assert has_www_auth or has_outcome, (
        "Response must include WWW-Authenticate header or FHIR OperationOutcome"
    )
